name: Google Sites
description: Google's website-building tool for creating simple internal and public sites without code, part of Google Workspace.

years.notes: First Material Design product icon for Google Sites (in use 2014–2020). Indigo file silhouette with a lighter-indigo folded corner and white wireframe page glyph (header bar + two content blocks). Subtle Material shading dropped to match project conventions; silhouette transcribed verbatim from Wikimedia Commons SVG.

2020 Workspace refresh paired with the G Suite → Google Workspace rebrand. Brighter indigo body (#4758B5), darker folded corner (#354287), white page-layout glyph. Sites kept the file-silhouette language rather than adopting the four-color outline used by Gmail/Calendar/Drive. Transcribed from Wikimedia Commons SVG (originally 64×88) into a 24×24 viewBox.

2026 Workspace refresh — Google Sites mark debuts a binder/document silhouette in saturated Workspace blues (#3186FF body, #50A0FF lower fill, #9ED1FF left binder strip) with a vertical gradient binder ring (#3186FF→#A9A9FD) and a white pill detail on the right. Geometry transcribed verbatim from the community Figma file 'New Google Workspace Icons 2026', exported via the Figma Desktop Bridge as a 24×24 SVG_STRING.
